referenced_message.author: caelancromwell
referenced_message.content:
I have 48.76% damage reduction. My weapon has  9.9% damage reduction but my total only 53.84%

That usually means the game is using multiplicative stacking for Damage Reduction (DR), not simple addition.

If it were additive:

Current DR = 48.76%
Weapon DR = 9.9%

Expected:

48.76% + 9.9% = 58.66%

But you're seeing 53.84%, which suggests diminishing returns.

Using multiplicative DR:

Remaining damage after 48.76% DR = 51.24% (0.5124)
Weapon reduces that remaining damage by 9.9%

Calculation:

1−(1−0.4876)(1−0.099)

1−(1−0.4876)(1−0.099)

Result:

1−(0.5124×0.901)=1−0.4617=53.83%

That's essentially the 53.84% you're seeing.

How much DR did the weapon actually add?
Before: 48.76%
After: 53.84%

Increase:

53.84 − 48.76 = 5.08 percentage points

So although the weapon says 9.9% DR, it only adds about 5.08% to your displayed DR because it's reducing the damage that remains after your existing DR is applied.

This is why getting from 50% to 75% DR is much harder than it looks—you need increasingly larger amounts of DR stats to raise the displayed percentage.

WHY EVERYBODY TALKS ABOUT COMBAT MEDIC?!?!?

James, please let dev team give some love to OXI Medic!

Corrosion "thing" in real life is - duh, corrosion? "Corrosion is the natural process where a refined metal gradually deteriorates" - Google

I understand that ALL people cannot penetrate "damage reduction" on max values - but if there is a spec which SHOULD ignore armor, reductions and such - that's corrosion medic, oxi medic, tactical pharma.

His heal is ass - if you posion 10 bots maybe you will get 1k HP per tick, but often this heal is silly, if you don't specificaly sacrifise DPS for Survivability (a lot), oxi medic dies in a 0.00003 seconds both in PVP and PVE missions

His damage works - but mostly in PVE, while in PVP damage reduction eats majority of damage by silly numbers.

I just feel like oxi medic don't have much love.

It should either - have some passive that let's you ignore at least some kind of defense, both for bots and players

OR - be "debuff" centered, like for example - having 40 corrosion stacks on enemies gives -10% damage reduction/-10% damage, or slows down your target, or something like that

Spec was created in heavens to be debuff oriented, but solely centered about pure damage..
